report_name: ARR Waterfall (Starting → New → Expansion → Churn → End)
primary_decisions_supported: Setting targets for new vs expansion sales
executive_questions_answered: How much ARR did we add this period from new customers vs existing (expansion)?
How much ARR was lost to churn or contraction?
What is our net ARR growth after accounting for churn and downgrades?

metric_definitions.definition: Net new recurring revenue added in the period: New logo ARR plus Expansion ARR minus ARR lost to churn and downgrades.
metric_definitions.formula: Net ARR Added = New_ARR + Expansion_ARR - Churned_ARR - Downgraded_ARR

known_variants.definition_delta: Counts only ARR gained (new + expansion) without subtracting churn (for sales-only view)
known_variants.when_to_use: Assessing sales performance ignoring churn impact

pitfalls: Net ARR can mask churn issues if expansions from a few customers offset broad churn
Contraction (downgrades) should be included with churn for full loss picture
Ensure consistent period – include only churn that occurred in that period's starting ARR cohort

metric_definitions.definition: Breakout of Starting ARR, New ARR, Expansion ARR, Churned ARR, and Ending ARR to visualize how each component contributes to net ARR change (waterfall chart).
metric_definitions.formula: Ending ARR = Starting ARR + New ARR + Expansion ARR - Churned ARR

known_variants.definition_delta: Uses monthly recurring revenue components for finer granularity within quarter
known_variants.when_to_use: Monthly internal reviews or where ARR jumps mid-period need breakdown

pitfalls: Components must align to same timeframe (e.g., include only churn that belongs to starting ARR of period)
Excluding downgrades (contraction) will overstate retention component
Too many minor categories make the chart hard to read – stick to major buckets

required_fields.description: ARR per account at period start
ARR from new customers closed in period
ARR added via upsell/cross-sell in period
ARR lost from customer churn or downgrades in period

joins_and_keys.notes: Identify new vs expansion deals by account status
Link subscriptions to accounts to aggregate churn and expansions

transformation_rules: Annualize partial-period contributions if needed (e.g., if a deal starts halfway through period, count full ARR for ARR metrics but note for revenue)
Exclude one-time fees or services from ARR components
Represent churn and downgrades as negative values for clarity in calculations

data_quality_checks.logic: Verify Starting ARR + New + Expansion - Churn = Ending ARR (consistency check across data sources)
Ensure every closed deal is tagged as New or Expansion (to correctly categorize New vs Expansion ARR)

derived_fields: Total_New_ARR = sum(ARR of new-logo deals in period)
Total_Expansion_ARR = sum(ARR of upsell deals in period)
Total_Churned_ARR = sum(ARR of all lost subscriptions in period)
aggregation_rules: Aggregate ARR components across all customers for company-level waterfall
For segments, calculate component sums per segment then aggregate for total (ensuring no double count)

layout.purpose: Visualize starting ARR to ending ARR with components of growth and churn
drilldowns: by region or product line to see component contributions
annotations_or_thresholds: Net ARR added vs plan target

layout.purpose: Tabular view of ARR changes by account or segment for auditing

narrative_insights_to_generate: Quantify net ARR added and identify whether growth was driven more by new business or expansion
Highlight the impact of churn – e.g., what percent of new ARR was eaten up by churn
diagnostic_questions: Which component (new vs expansion vs churn) had the largest impact on ARR this period?
Is expansion revenue sufficiently offsetting churn compared to last period?

recommended_actions.action: Invest in retention programs if churn high
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: Churned ARR exceeds X% of starting ARR (waterfall shows net negative or weak net growth)
Expansion ARR portion is below plan or below historical norm

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Single-period waterfall with total new, expansion, churn values
implementation_notes.advanced_version: Dynamic waterfall over multiple periods with drilldowns by segment/product and scenario overlays
common_client_customizations: Separate churn into voluntary vs involuntary or by reason in the chart
Include reactivation ARR (if lost customers resubscribe) as a positive component
privacy_and_access_notes: Only share account-level breakdown internally; external reports (e.g., board) see aggregated components
Ensure all revenue figures align with official financials for external use
success_criteria: ARR waterfall ties exactly to known ARR values (reconcilable with CRM and Finance records)
Leaders can easily identify if growth is primarily new logo-driven or expansion-driven each period

references.title: Net Annual Recurring Revenue Added (Net ARR Added)
